AI-Powered Engineering Drawing Change Detection System¶

Automated Comparison of Engineering Drawings using Computer Vision and OCR

Author

Author: Ramu Gopal

Senior Mechanical Design Engineer | CAD Automation Specialist

Project

Project: AI-Assisted Engineering Drawing Change Detection System

This notebook demonstrates a prototype system for automatically detecting differences between engineering drawing revisions using computer vision, OCR text extraction, and engineering token analysis.

Developed By

Developed by Ramu Gopal, founder of The Tech Thinker, a technology platform focused on:

  • CAD automation

  • engineering productivity tools

  • AI applications in engineering workflows

  • Digital Engineering

Platform

  • Project published as part of the The Tech Thinker knowledge platform.

Website: The Tech Thinker

Keywords

Engineering drawing comparison, CAD automation, drawing checker, computer vision for engineering, OCR drawing analysis.

1. Introduction

Engineering drawings are the primary communication medium in mechanical design and manufacturing. During product development, drawings are frequently revised to incorporate design improvements, manufacturing feedback, or engineering corrections.

However, identifying the exact changes between two drawing revisions can be difficult and time-consuming. Engineers often compare drawings manually, scanning multiple views, annotations, and notes to detect modifications.

This manual process introduces several challenges:

  • High risk of overlooking small changes

  • Time-consuming review process

  • Inconsistent interpretation across engineers

  • Difficulty documenting revision differences

To address these issues, this project proposes an AI-assisted drawing comparison system that automatically analyzes two engineering drawings and identifies potential differences using computer vision and OCR techniques.

2. Problem Statement

In many engineering organizations, revision checking is still performed manually by engineers or quality reviewers.

Typical workflow:

  • Open the previous drawing revision.

  • Open the new revision.

  • Compare views, annotations, dimensions, and notes.

  • Manually identify changes.

This process becomes inefficient when:

  • Drawings contain multiple views

  • Revisions are frequent

  • Minor changes occur in annotations or notes

  • Large drawing sets must be validated

Therefore, an automated method is required to:

  • Detect visual differences between drawing revisions

  • Identify annotation and dimension changes

  • Extract and compare engineering tokens from OCR text

  • Provide structured change summaries

3. Objective of the Project

The objective of this project is to develop a prototype automated drawing checker capable of comparing two engineering drawings and identifying potential changes.

The system aims to:

  • Align two drawing revisions for accurate comparison

  • Detect visual differences using pixel analysis

  • Extract engineering text using OCR

  • Identify token-level changes such as:

  • labels

  • dimensions

  • angles

  • chamfers

  • Generate a structured engineering change report

  • Evaluate detection performance using standard metrics

4. Scope of the System

The current prototype focuses on a limited set of drawing components.

The system analyzes the following regions:

  • Section views

  • Front views

  • Side views

  • Isometric views

  • Notes block

Detected change types include:

  • Label additions or removals

  • Dimension additions or removals

  • Possible dimension modifications

  • Notes changes

The system produces a structured summary for each Region of Interest (ROI).

5. Methodology Overview

The proposed drawing comparison system follows a multi-stage processing pipeline.

Main stages include:

  • Drawing Image Preprocessing

  • Drawing Alignment

  • Global Pixel Difference Detection

  • Region of Interest (ROI) Extraction

  • OCR Text Extraction

  • Engineering Token Extraction

  • Token Change Analysis

  • Engineering Change Interpretation

  • Evaluation using Ground Truth

This structured approach combines computer vision techniques with text-based analysis to improve detection reliability.

6. System Architecture

The system consists of the following functional modules:

  • Image preprocessing module

  • Drawing alignment module

  • ROI detection module

  • OCR text extraction module

  • Engineering token extraction module

  • Token comparison module

  • Engineering change reporting module

  • Evaluation module

These modules collectively form an automated pipeline for drawing revision comparison.

7. Evaluation Approach

To assess the effectiveness of the system, a ground truth dataset was manually defined for the test drawing pair.

  • Evaluation metrics include:

  • ROI change detection accuracy

  • Change-type classification accuracy

  • Engineering token extraction accuracy

  • Precision, recall, and F1 score

The evaluation provides a quantitative measure of the system’s ability to detect engineering drawing changes.

8. Expected Output

The system generates a structured engineering change report for each drawing region.

Typical outputs include:

  • Detected label additions or removals

  • Possible dimension modifications

  • Token-level annotation differences

  • ROI change confidence levels

The system also produces evaluation tables and visualizations such as:

  • confusion matrix

  • detection metrics

  • ROI outcome charts

9. Applications

This automated drawing checker concept can be applied in several engineering workflows:

  • Design revision verification

  • Manufacturing drawing validation

  • Engineering change management

  • Quality inspection of drawings

  • Automated documentation review

With further development, such systems could integrate with CAD platforms or PLM systems to assist engineers during design review processes.

In [18]:
from PIL import Image
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def show(img_path, title):
    img = Image.open(img_path)
    plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))
    plt.imshow(img)
    plt.axis("off")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.show()

if min_pages > 0:
    show(old_pages[0], "OLD - Page 1")
    show(new_pages[0], "NEW - Page 1")

Step 11 – Define Image Alignment Functio¶

In [19]:
import cv2
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

ALIGNED_DIR = BASE / "output" / "aligned"
ALIGNED_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def align_images(old_img_path, new_img_path, out_path):
    old_img = cv2.imread(str(old_img_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
    new_img = cv2.imread(str(new_img_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)

    # ORB detector
    orb = cv2.ORB_create(5000)
    kp1, des1 = orb.detectAndCompute(old_img, None)
    kp2, des2 = orb.detectAndCompute(new_img, None)

    # Matcher
    bf = cv2.BFMatcher(cv2.NORM_HAMMING, crossCheck=True)
    matches = bf.match(des1, des2)
    matches = sorted(matches, key=lambda x: x.distance)

    good_matches = matches[:150]

    pts1 = np.float32([kp1[m.queryIdx].pt for m in good_matches]).reshape(-1,1,2)
    pts2 = np.float32([kp2[m.trainIdx].pt for m in good_matches]).reshape(-1,1,2)

    # Homography
    H, _ = cv2.findHomography(pts2, pts1, cv2.RANSAC, 5.0)

    height, width = old_img.shape
    aligned = cv2.warpPerspective(cv2.imread(str(new_img_path)), H, (width, height))

    cv2.imwrite(str(out_path), aligned)
    return out_path

Step 12 – Execute Drawing Alignment¶

In [20]:
aligned_p1 = ALIGNED_DIR / "aligned_p001.png"

out = align_images(old_pages[0], new_pages[0], aligned_p1)
print("Aligned output:", out)

Step 15 – Define Geometry Difference Detection Function¶

In [23]:
import cv2
import numpy as np

def generate_diff(old_img, aligned_img, diff_mask_path, annotated_path):
    # Read images
    img_old = cv2.imread(str(old_img), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
    img_new = cv2.imread(str(aligned_img), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)

    # Smooth (reduces noise)
    old_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(img_old, (5,5), 0)
    new_blur = cv2.GaussianBlur(img_new, (5,5), 0)

    # Absolute diff → main engine
    diff = cv2.absdiff(old_blur, new_blur)

    # Threshold to binary
    _, thresh = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

    # Morph (clean)
    kernel = np.ones((5,5), np.uint8)
    clean = cv2.morphologyEx(thresh, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)
    clean = cv2.dilate(clean, kernel, iterations=1)

    # Save diff mask
    cv2.imwrite(str(diff_mask_path), clean)

    # Create red overlay annotation
    color_new = cv2.cvtColor(img_new, cv2.COLOR_GRAY2BGR)
    overlay = color_new.copy()
    overlay[clean > 0] = (0,0,255)   # RED highlights

    alpha = 0.65
    annotated = cv2.addWeighted(color_new, alpha, overlay, 1 - alpha, 0)

    cv2.imwrite(str(annotated_path), annotated)

    return diff_mask_path, annotated_path

Step 16 – Generate Geometry Difference Mask and Annotation¶

In [24]:
DIFF_P1 = GEOM_DIR / "p001_mask.png"
ANN_P1  = ANN_DIR  / "p001_annotated.png"

generate_diff(old_pages[0], aligned_p1, DIFF_P1, ANN_P1)

print("Diff Mask:", DIFF_P1)
print("Annotated:", ANN_P1)

Step 18 – Define ROI Templates for Drawing Regions¶

In [26]:
ROI_TEMPLATE = {
    "section_view":      {"x1": 0.30, "y1": 0.34, "x2": 0.66, "y2": 0.76},
    "front_face_view":   {"x1": 0.03, "y1": 0.58, "x2": 0.24, "y2": 0.95},
    "front_side_view":   {"x1": 0.23, "y1": 0.60, "x2": 0.32, "y2": 0.93},
    "iso_view":          {"x1": 0.74, "y1": 0.56, "x2": 0.95, "y2": 0.80},
    "notes_block":       {"x1": 0.58, "y1": 0.82, "x2": 0.90, "y2": 0.94}
}

Step 19 – Visualize ROI Template on Drawing¶

In [27]:
import cv2
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from PIL import Image

img = cv2.imread(str(aligned_p1))
h, w = img.shape[:2]

for name, box in ROI_TEMPLATE.items():
    x1 = int(box["x1"] * w)
    y1 = int(box["y1"] * h)
    x2 = int(box["x2"] * w)
    y2 = int(box["y2"] * h)
    cv2.rectangle(img, (x1,y1), (x2,y2), (0,0,255), 2)
    cv2.putText(img, name, (x1, y1-5), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.6, (0,0,255), 2)

plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
plt.imshow(cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB))
plt.axis("off")
plt.title("ROI Template (Visual Check)")
plt.show()

Step 20 – Define ROI-Based Difference Function¶

In [28]:
def roi_diff(aligned_img_path, old_img_path, roi_box, out_path):
    # load images
    old = cv2.imread(str(old_img_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
    new = cv2.imread(str(aligned_img_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)

    h, w = old.shape[:2]

    # extract ROI region
    x1 = int(roi_box["x1"] * w)
    y1 = int(roi_box["y1"] * h)
    x2 = int(roi_box["x2"] * w)
    y2 = int(roi_box["y2"] * h)

    old_roi = old[y1:y2, x1:x2]
    new_roi = new[y1:y2, x1:x2]

    # absolute diff
    diff = cv2.absdiff(old_roi, new_roi)
    _, mask = cv2.threshold(diff, 30, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

    # cleanup noise
    kernel = np.ones((3,3),np.uint8)
    mask = cv2.morphologyEx(mask, cv2.MORPH_OPEN, kernel)
    mask = cv2.dilate(mask, kernel, iterations=1)

    cv2.imwrite(str(out_path), mask)

    # --- NEW PART ---
    changed_pixels = np.sum(mask > 0)
    total_pixels = mask.size
    change_percent = (changed_pixels / total_pixels) * 100

    return out_path, change_percent

Step 21 – Execute ROI-Based Difference Analysis¶

In [29]:
roi = ROI_TEMPLATE

roi_output_dir = BASE / "output" / "roi_diff"
roi_output_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

roi_results = {}
roi_change_stats = {}

for name, box in roi.items():
    out_file = roi_output_dir / f"p001_{name}.png"
    saved_file, change_percent = roi_diff(aligned_p1, old_pages[0], box, out_file)
    roi_results[name] = saved_file
    roi_change_stats[name] = round(change_percent, 2)

print("ROI diff files:")
print(roi_results)

print("\nROI change percentages:")
for k, v in roi_change_stats.items():
    print(f"{k:15s} : {v:.2f}%")

ROI diff files:
{'section_view': PosixPath('/content/drive/MyDrive/Drawing_Checker/output/roi_diff/p001_section_view.png'), 'front_face_view': PosixPath('/content/drive/MyDrive/Drawing_Checker/output/roi_diff/p001_front_face_view.png'), 'front_side_view': PosixPath('/content/drive/MyDrive/Drawing_Checker/output/roi_diff/p001_front_side_view.png'), 'iso_view': PosixPath('/content/drive/MyDrive/Drawing_Checker/output/roi_diff/p001_iso_view.png'), 'notes_block': PosixPath('/content/drive/MyDrive/Drawing_Checker/output/roi_diff/p001_notes_block.png')}

front_face_view : 0.21%
front_side_view : 0.17%
iso_view        : 0.11%
notes_block     : 6.75%

Step 22 – Visualize ROI Difference Results¶

In [30]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from PIL import Image
import math

files = list(roi_results.items())
n = len(files)

cols = 3
rows = math.ceil(n / cols)

plt.figure(figsize=(16, 5 * rows))

for i, (name, file) in enumerate(files, start=1):
    plt.subplot(rows, cols, i)
    img = Image.open(file)
    plt.imshow(img, cmap='gray')

    pct = roi_change_stats.get(name, 0)
    plt.title(f"{name}  |  {pct:.2f}% change")

    plt.axis("off")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

 Found libarchive 3.6.0 zlib/1.2.11 liblzma/5.2.5 bz2lib/1.0.8 liblz4/1.9.3 libzstd/1.4.8

Step 24 – Define OCR Extraction Function for ROI Text¶

In [32]:
import pytesseract
import cv2
import numpy as np

def ocr_roi(image_path):

    img = cv2.imread(str(image_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)

    # enlarge (important for CAD fonts)
    img = cv2.resize(img, None, fx=2.0, fy=2.0, interpolation=cv2.INTER_CUBIC)

    # light denoise (keep thin lines)
    img = cv2.bilateralFilter(img, 9, 75, 75)

    # adaptive threshold works better for drawings
    img = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(
        img,
        255,
        cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_GAUSSIAN_C,
        cv2.THRESH_BINARY,
        31,
        5
    )

    config = "--oem 3 --psm 6"
    text = pytesseract.image_to_string(img, config=config)

    return text.strip()

Step 25 – Define ROI Cropping Function¶

In [33]:
import cv2
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

def crop_roi(img_path, roi_box):
    img = cv2.imread(str(img_path), cv2.IMREAD_GRAYSCALE)
    h, w = img.shape[:2]

    x1 = int(roi_box["x1"] * w)
    y1 = int(roi_box["y1"] * h)
    x2 = int(roi_box["x2"] * w)
    y2 = int(roi_box["y2"] * h)

    roi = img[y1:y2, x1:x2]
    return roi

Step 26 – Define Enhanced OCR Function for CAD Text¶

In [35]:
import pytesseract

def ocr_image(gray_roi):
    # enlarge
    roi = cv2.resize(gray_roi, None, fx=2.0, fy=2.0, interpolation=cv2.INTER_CUBIC)

    # light denoise only
    roi = cv2.bilateralFilter(roi, 9, 75, 75)

    # adaptive threshold works better for drawings
    roi = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(roi, 255, cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_GAUSSIAN_C,
                                cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 31, 5)

    config = "--oem 3 --psm 6 -c tessedit_char_whitelist=0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ.-Ø°"
    text = pytesseract.image_to_string(roi, config=config)
    return text.strip()

Step 27 – Define OCR Function for Notes Block¶

In [36]:
def ocr_notes(gray_roi):
    roi = cv2.resize(gray_roi, None, fx=3.0, fy=3.0, interpolation=cv2.INTER_CUBIC)
    roi = cv2.GaussianBlur(roi, (3, 3), 0)

    # Try simple binary threshold for faint old-note text
    _, roi_bin = cv2.threshold(roi, 180, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)

    config = "--oem 3 --psm 6"
    text = pytesseract.image_to_string(roi_bin, config=config)
    return text.strip()

plt.figure(figsize=(10,3))
plt.imshow(roi_img, cmap="gray")
plt.axis("off")
plt.title(f"ROI crop preview: {roi_name}")
plt.show()

Step 29 – Extract OCR Text from Drawing Notes Block¶

In [38]:
ocr_output_old = {}
ocr_output_new = {}

OCR_ROIS = ["notes_block"]

for name in OCR_ROIS:
    box = ROI_TEMPLATE[name]
    old_roi = crop_roi(old_pages[0], box)
    new_roi = crop_roi(aligned_p1, box)

    if name == "notes_block":
        ocr_output_old[name] = ocr_notes(old_roi)
        ocr_output_new[name] = ocr_notes(new_roi)
    else:
        ocr_output_old[name] = ocr_image(old_roi)
        ocr_output_new[name] = ocr_image(new_roi)

for key in OCR_ROIS:
    print("\n======= ", key, " =======")
    print("OLD:\n", ocr_output_old[key])
    print("\nNEW:\n", ocr_output_new[key])

=======  notes_block  =======
OLD:
 

NEW:
 1. Read this drawing with previous number
2. Do not weld should be done at shop

Step 30 – Normalize OCR Text and Compute Similarity¶

In [39]:
import re
from difflib import SequenceMatcher

def normalize_text(s: str) -> str:
    if not s:
        return ""
    s = s.upper()
    s = s.replace("|", " ")
    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\.\-\[\]\/]", " ", s)   # keep basic tokens
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

def similarity(a: str, b: str) -> float:
    a2, b2 = normalize_text(a), normalize_text(b)
    if not a2 and not b2:
        return 1.0
    return SequenceMatcher(None, a2, b2).ratio()

Step 31 – Compute OCR Similarity Scores for ROIs¶

In [40]:
roi_keys = OCR_ROIS

rows = []
for k in roi_keys:
    old_t = ocr_output_old.get(k, "")
    new_t = ocr_output_new.get(k, "")
    sim = similarity(old_t, new_t)
    rows.append((k, round(sim*100, 2)))

rows
Out[40]:
[('notes_block', 0.0)]

Step 32 – Evaluate OCR Similarity and Change Status¶

In [41]:
for k, pct in rows:
    status = "PASS" if pct >= 95 else "CHANGE"
    print(f"{k:15s}  Similarity: {pct:6.2f}%   => {status}")

notes_block      Similarity:   0.00%   => CHANGE

Step 33 – Generate OCR Change Summary¶

In [42]:
summary = []

for key in ocr_output_old.keys():

    old_text = ocr_output_old.get(key, "")
    new_text = ocr_output_new.get(key, "")

    sim = similarity(old_text, new_text)

    if sim < 0.95:
        summary.append(f"{key} changed (similarity {round(sim*100,2)}%)")
    else:
        summary.append(f"{key} no major change")

print("\n✅ Auto Summary:")
for s in summary:
    print("-", s)

✅ Auto Summary:
- notes_block changed (similarity 0.0%)

Step 34 – Classify Notes Changes Based on OCR Comparison¶

In [43]:
roi_keys = OCR_ROIS

rows = []
for k in roi_keys:
    old_t = ocr_output_old.get(k, "").strip()
    new_t = ocr_output_new.get(k, "").strip()

    if not old_t and not new_t:
        status = "NO NOTES / NO CHANGE"
        sim_pct = 100.0
    elif not old_t and new_t:
        status = "NOTES ADDED"
        sim_pct = 0.0
    elif old_t and not new_t:
        status = "NOTES REMOVED"
        sim_pct = 0.0
    else:
        sim = similarity(old_t, new_t)
        sim_pct = round(sim * 100, 2)
        status = "NOTES SAME" if sim_pct >= 95 else "NOTES MODIFIED"

    rows.append((k, sim_pct, status))

rows
Out[43]:
[('notes_block', 0.0, 'NOTES ADDED')]

    pixel_pct = roi_change_stats.get(name, 0.0)

    print(f"ROI Name         : {name}")
    print(f"Pixel Change %   : {pixel_pct:.2f}%")
    print(f"Text Similarity  : {sim_pct:.2f}%")
    print(f"Notes Status     : {status}")
    print("-" * 50)

Step 41 – Define Robust OCR Function for View ROI Tokens¶

In [50]:
#Robust OCR for view ROIs (dimensions / labels / angles / diameter)

import cv2
import pytesseract
import re

def ocr_view_tokens(gray_roi):
    results = []

    scales = [2.0, 3.0, 4.0]
    psm_modes = [6, 11, 12]

    for sc in scales:
        roi = cv2.resize(gray_roi, None, fx=sc, fy=sc, interpolation=cv2.INTER_CUBIC)

        variants = []

        # Variant 1: simple binary
        _, th1 = cv2.threshold(roi, 180, 255, cv2.THRESH_BINARY)
        variants.append(th1)

        # Variant 2: adaptive threshold
        th2 = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(
            roi, 255,
            cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_GAUSSIAN_C,
            cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 31, 5
        )
        variants.append(th2)

        # Variant 3: denoise + adaptive
        den = cv2.bilateralFilter(roi, 9, 75, 75)
        th3 = cv2.adaptiveThreshold(
            den, 255,
            cv2.ADAPTIVE_THRESH_GAUSSIAN_C,
            cv2.THRESH_BINARY, 31, 5
        )
        variants.append(th3)

        for img in variants:
            for psm in psm_modes:
                config = f"--oem 3 --psm {psm} -c tessedit_char_whitelist=0123456789ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz.-ØR°xX():[]/"
                txt = pytesseract.image_to_string(img, config=config).strip()
                if txt:
                    results.append(txt)

    return results

Step 42 – Extract Engineering Tokens from OCR Text¶

In [122]:
# Purpose:
#   Extract engineering tokens from OCR text and clean noisy dimension values.
# Inputs:
#   texts -> list of OCR text strings
# Outputs:
#   token dictionary with labels, diameter, radius, angle, linear, chamfer

import re

def normalize_eng_text(s: str) -> str:
    if not s:
        return ""
    s = s.replace("∅", "Ø").replace("φ", "Ø").replace("0/", "Ø").replace("O/", "Ø")
    s = s.replace("deg", "°").replace("DEG", "°")
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s


def extract_engineering_tokens(texts):
    if texts is None:
        texts = []

    joined = " | ".join([normalize_eng_text(t) for t in texts if t])

    tokens = {
        "labels": sorted(set(re.findall(r"\b[A-Z]-[A-Z]\b|\b[A-Z]\s*\(\s*\d+\s*:\s*\d+\s*\)", joined))),
        "diameter": sorted(set(re.findall(r"Ø\s*\d+(?:\.\d+)?", joined))),
        "radius": sorted(set(re.findall(r"R\s*\d+(?:\.\d+)?", joined))),
        "angle": sorted(set(re.findall(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*°", joined))),
        "linear": sorted(set(re.findall(r"(?<![A-ZØR])\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\b", joined))),
        "chamfer": sorted(set(re.findall(r"\d+\s*[xX]\s*\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*°", joined))),
    }

    # clean spaces inside tokens
    for k in tokens:
        tokens[k] = [re.sub(r"\s+", "", x) for x in tokens[k]]

    return tokens


def filter_dimension_noise(tokens):
    if tokens is None:
        return {
            "labels": [],
            "diameter": [],
            "radius": [],
            "angle": [],
            "linear": [],
            "chamfer": []
        }

    clean = {k: list(v) for k, v in tokens.items()}

    filtered_linear = []
    for x in clean.get("linear", []):
        try:
            val = float(x)
            if len(str(x)) >= 2 and val < 2000:
                filtered_linear.append(x)
        except:
            pass

    clean["linear"] = filtered_linear
    return clean

STEP 43 — Dimension and Label Token Comparison for Changed ROI¶

test_roi = "front_side_view"

new_img = view_roi_crops[test_roi]["new"]

old_texts = ocr_view_tokens(old_img)
new_texts = ocr_view_tokens(new_img)

old_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(old_texts)
new_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(new_texts)

old_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(old_tokens_raw)
new_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(new_tokens_raw)

STEP 44 — Engineering Token Change Detection (Added / Removed / Common)¶

In [121]:
# Purpose:
#   Identify added / removed / unchanged engineering tokens for one ROI.
# Inputs:
#   old_tokens, new_tokens ->
# Outputs:
#   comparison dictionary with per-category changes

def compare_token_sets(old_tokens, new_tokens):
    categories = ["labels", "diameter", "radius", "angle", "linear", "chamfer"]

    report = {}

    for cat in categories:
        old_set = set(old_tokens.get(cat, []))
        new_set = set(new_tokens.get(cat, []))

        report[cat] = {
            "removed": sorted(list(old_set - new_set)),
            "added": sorted(list(new_set - old_set)),
            "common": sorted(list(old_set & new_set))
        }

    return report


token_report = compare_token_sets(old_tokens, new_tokens)

print("ROI:", test_roi)
print("\nTOKEN CHANGE REPORT:")
for cat, info in token_report.items():
    print(f"\n[{cat.upper()}]")
    print("  Removed:", info["removed"])
    print("  Added  :", info["added"])
    print("  Common :", info["common"])

ROI: front_side_view

TOKEN CHANGE REPORT:

[LABELS]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

[DIAMETER]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

[RADIUS]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

[ANGLE]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

[LINEAR]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

[CHAMFER]
  Removed: []
  Added  : []
  Common : []

STEP 45 — Engineering Token Change Confidence Assessment¶

In [59]:
# Purpose:
#   Convert token difference report into human-readable engineering interpretation.
# Inputs:
#   token_report ->
#   old_texts, new_texts -> OCR candidate lists
# Outputs:
#   interpretation dictionary

def assess_token_change_confidence(token_report, old_texts, new_texts):
    total_removed = sum(len(v["removed"]) for v in token_report.values())
    total_added = sum(len(v["added"]) for v in token_report.values())

    old_count = len(old_texts) if old_texts else 0
    new_count = len(new_texts) if new_texts else 0

    noisy_old = any(("[" in str(x) or "]" in str(x)) for x in (old_texts or []))
    noisy_new = any(("[" in str(x) or "]" in str(x)) for x in (new_texts or []))

    if total_removed == 0 and total_added == 0:
        status = "NO TOKEN CHANGE"
        confidence = "HIGH"
        remark = "Same engineering tokens found in both old and new ROI."
    elif noisy_old or noisy_new:
        status = "POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE"
        confidence = "LOW"
        remark = "OCR noise detected. Token difference may be caused by OCR instability."
    elif old_count >= 5 and new_count >= 5:
        status = "LIKELY TOKEN CHANGE"
        confidence = "MEDIUM"
        remark = "Multiple OCR candidates found. Token difference may indicate a real annotation change."
    else:
        status = "POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE"
        confidence = "LOW"
        remark = "Limited OCR evidence. Manual review recommended."

    return {
        "status": status,
        "confidence": confidence,
        "remark": remark
    }


confidence_report = assess_token_change_confidence(token_report, old_texts, new_texts)

print("\nCONFIDENCE REPORT:")
for k, v in confidence_report.items():
    print(f"{k}: {v}")

CONFIDENCE REPORT:
status: POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
confidence: LOW
remark: OCR noise detected. Token difference may be caused by OCR instability.

STEP 46 — Build Structured ROI Engineering Change Summary¶

In [60]:
# Purpose:
#   Convert token comparison + confidence result into a structured ROI report.
# Inputs:
#   roi_name, pixel_change_percent, token_report, confidence_report
# Outputs:
#   roi_summary dictionary

def build_roi_summary(roi_name, pixel_change_percent, token_report, confidence_report):
    total_removed = sum(len(v["removed"]) for v in token_report.values())
    total_added = sum(len(v["added"]) for v in token_report.values())

    changed_categories = []
    for cat, info in token_report.items():
        if info["removed"] or info["added"]:
            changed_categories.append(cat)

    remarks = []

    if "linear" in changed_categories:
        removed_linear = token_report["linear"]["removed"]
        added_linear = token_report["linear"]["added"]

        if removed_linear:
            remarks.append(f"Possible removed linear token(s): {removed_linear}")
        if added_linear:
            remarks.append(f"Possible added linear token(s): {added_linear}")

    if "labels" in changed_categories:
        if token_report["labels"]["removed"]:
            remarks.append(f"Possible removed label(s): {token_report['labels']['removed']}")
        if token_report["labels"]["added"]:
            remarks.append(f"Possible added label(s): {token_report['labels']['added']}")

    if not remarks:
        remarks.append("No strong token-level annotation difference detected.")

    roi_summary = {
        "roi_name": roi_name,
        "pixel_change_percent": pixel_change_percent,
        "changed_categories": changed_categories,
        "total_removed_tokens": total_removed,
        "total_added_tokens": total_added,
        "token_report": token_report,
        "status": confidence_report["status"],
        "confidence": confidence_report["confidence"],
        "remark": confidence_report["remark"],
        "engineering_note": " | ".join(remarks)
    }

    return roi_summary

STEP 47 — Generate and Display Structured ROI Change Summary¶

In [61]:
pixel_change_percent = roi_change_stats.get(test_roi, 0.0)

roi_summary = build_roi_summary(
    roi_name=test_roi,
    pixel_change_percent=pixel_change_percent,
    token_report=token_report,
    confidence_report=confidence_report
)

print("STRUCTURED ROI SUMMARY:\n")
for k, v in roi_summary.items():
    print(f"{k}: {v}")

STRUCTURED ROI SUMMARY:

roi_name: front_side_view
pixel_change_percent: 0.17
changed_categories: ['linear']
total_removed_tokens: 1
total_added_tokens: 0
token_report: {'labels': {'removed': [], 'added': [], 'common': []}, 'diameter': {'removed': [], 'added': [], 'common': []}, 'radius': {'removed': [], 'added': [], 'common': []}, 'angle': {'removed': [], 'added': [], 'common': ['45°']}, 'linear': {'removed': ['26'], 'added': [], 'common': []}, 'chamfer': {'removed': [], 'added': [], 'common': ['6x45°']}}
status: POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
confidence: LOW
remark: OCR noise detected. Token difference may be caused by OCR instability.
engineering_note: Possible removed linear token(s): ['26']

STEP 48 — Run Full ROI Engineering Change Analysis Pipeline¶

In [62]:
# Purpose:
#   Run OCR token extraction, comparison, confidence assessment, and summary building
#   for each candidate ROI.
# Inputs:
#   candidate_view_rois, view_roi_crops, roi_change_stats
# Outputs:
#   final_roi_summaries dictionary

final_roi_summaries = {}

for roi_name in candidate_view_rois:
    old_img = view_roi_crops[roi_name]["old"]
    new_img = view_roi_crops[roi_name]["new"]

    old_texts = ocr_view_tokens(old_img)
    new_texts = ocr_view_tokens(new_img)

    old_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(old_texts)
    new_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(new_texts)

    old_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(old_tokens_raw)
    new_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(new_tokens_raw)

    token_report = compare_token_sets(old_tokens, new_tokens)
    confidence_report = assess_token_change_confidence(token_report, old_texts, new_texts)

    pixel_change_percent = roi_change_stats.get(roi_name, 0.0)

    roi_summary = build_roi_summary(
        roi_name=roi_name,
        pixel_change_percent=pixel_change_percent,
        token_report=token_report,
        confidence_report=confidence_report
    )

    roi_summary["old_texts_preview"] = old_texts[:5] if old_texts else []
    roi_summary["new_texts_preview"] = new_texts[:5] if new_texts else []
    roi_summary["old_tokens"] = old_tokens
    roi_summary["new_tokens"] = new_tokens

    final_roi_summaries[roi_name] = roi_summary

print("STEP 48 complete.")
print("ROIs processed:", list(final_roi_summaries.keys()))

STEP 54 complete.
ROIs processed: ['section_view', 'front_face_view', 'front_side_view', 'iso_view']

STEP 49 — Display Consolidated ROI Engineering Change Summary¶

In [63]:
# Purpose:
#   Show a readable summary of all ROI-level results.
# Inputs:
#   final_roi_summaries
# Outputs:
#   printed summary report

for roi_name, info in final_roi_summaries.items():
    print("\n" + "="*70)
    print("ROI:", roi_name)
    print("Pixel Change %:", info["pixel_change_percent"])
    print("Status:", info["status"])
    print("Confidence:", info["confidence"])
    print("Changed Categories:", info["changed_categories"])
    print("Engineering Note:", info["engineering_note"])

STEP 50 — Categorize ROI Results into Engineering Review Buckets¶

In [64]:
# Purpose:
#   Group ROI summaries into practical engineering review categories.
# Inputs:
#   final_roi_summaries
# Outputs:
#   roi_buckets dictionary

roi_buckets = {
    "likely_no_change": [],
    "possible_change_low_conf": [],
    "likely_change_medium_or_high": []
}

for roi_name, info in final_roi_summaries.items():
    status = info["status"]
    confidence = info["confidence"]

    if status == "NO TOKEN CHANGE":
        roi_buckets["likely_no_change"].append(roi_name)
    elif confidence == "LOW":
        roi_buckets["possible_change_low_conf"].append(roi_name)
    else:
        roi_buckets["likely_change_medium_or_high"].append(roi_name)

print("ROI BUCKETS:\n")
for k, v in roi_buckets.items():
    print(k, ":", v)

ROI BUCKETS:

likely_no_change : ['iso_view']
possible_change_low_conf : ['section_view', 'front_side_view']
likely_change_medium_or_high : ['front_face_view']

STEP 51 — Detect Engineering Symbol Suspicion Flags from OCR Text¶

In [66]:
# Purpose:
#   Inspect OCR text candidates and raise lightweight suspicion flags
#   for weld-related, GD&T-related, section-label, and view-label patterns.
# Inputs:
#   texts -> list of OCR text strings
# Outputs:
#   flags dictionary with boolean indicators

import re

def symbol_suspicion_flags(texts):
    if texts is None:
        texts = []

    safe_texts = [str(t) for t in texts if t is not None]
    joined = " | ".join(safe_texts).upper()

    flags = {
        "weld_suspected": any(
            x in joined for x in ["WELD", "FILLET", "V-GROOVE", "GROOVE"]
        ),
        "gdt_suspected": any(
            x in joined for x in ["DATUM", "TRUE POSITION", "FLATNESS", "PROFILE", "RUNOUT"]
        ),
        "section_label_suspected": bool(
            re.search(r"\b[A-Z]-[A-Z]\b", joined)
        ),
        "view_label_suspected": bool(
            re.search(r"\b[A-Z]\s*\(\s*\d+\s*:\s*\d+\s*\)", joined)
        ),
    }

    return flags

STEP 52 — Validate and Display Engineering Symbol Suspicion Flags per ROI¶

In [67]:
# Inputs:
#   candidate_view_rois, view_roi_crops, ocr_view_tokens
# Outputs:
#   printed flag summary and basic validation status

required_flag_keys = {
    "weld_suspected",
    "gdt_suspected",
    "section_label_suspected",
    "view_label_suspected",
}

for roi_name in candidate_view_rois:
    old_texts = ocr_view_tokens(view_roi_crops[roi_name]["old"])
    new_texts = ocr_view_tokens(view_roi_crops[roi_name]["new"])

    old_flags = symbol_suspicion_flags(old_texts)
    new_flags = symbol_suspicion_flags(new_texts)

    old_ok = isinstance(old_flags, dict) and required_flag_keys.issubset(old_flags.keys())
    new_ok = isinstance(new_flags, dict) and required_flag_keys.issubset(new_flags.keys())

    print("\n" + "-" * 60)
    print("ROI:", roi_name)
    print("OLD FLAGS:", old_flags, "| VALID:", old_ok)
    print("NEW FLAGS:", new_flags, "| VALID:", new_ok)

------------------------------------------------------------

NEW FLAGS: {'weld_suspected': False, 'gdt_suspected': False, 'section_label_suspected': False, 'view_label_suspected': False} | VALID: True

------------------------------------------------------------
ROI: front_face_view
OLD FLAGS: {'weld_suspected': False, 'gdt_suspected': False, 'section_label_suspected': True, 'view_label_suspected': False} | VALID: True
NEW FLAGS: {'weld_suspected': False, 'gdt_suspected': False, 'section_label_suspected': True, 'view_label_suspected': True} | VALID: True

------------------------------------------------------------

NEW FLAGS: {'weld_suspected': False, 'gdt_suspected': False, 'section_label_suspected': False, 'view_label_suspected': False} | VALID: True

STEP 53 — Perform ROI-Level Engineering Token Difference Analysis¶

In [69]:
# Purpose:
#   Compare engineering tokens between old and new images for one ROI.
# Inputs:
#   test_roi, view_roi_crops
# Outputs:
#   printed old/new token sets and token difference report

test_roi = "front_side_view"

if test_roi not in view_roi_crops:
    raise ValueError(f"ROI '{test_roi}' not found in view_roi_crops")

old_img = view_roi_crops[test_roi].get("old", None)
new_img = view_roi_crops[test_roi].get("new", None)

if old_img is None or new_img is None:
    raise ValueError(f"Missing old/new image data for ROI '{test_roi}'")

old_texts = ocr_view_tokens(old_img)
new_texts = ocr_view_tokens(new_img)

old_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(old_texts)
new_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(new_texts)

old_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(old_tokens_raw)
new_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(new_tokens_raw)

token_report = compare_token_sets(old_tokens, new_tokens)

print("ROI:", test_roi)

print("\nOLD TOKENS:")
print(old_tokens)

print("\nNEW TOKENS:")
print(new_tokens)

print("\nTOKEN CHANGE REPORT:")

has_change = False
for k, v in token_report.items():
    if v["added"] or v["removed"]:
        has_change = True
        print(f"\n{k.upper()}:")
        if v["added"]:
            print("  added  :", v["added"])
        if v["removed"]:
            print("  removed:", v["removed"])

if not has_change:
    print("No token-level differences detected.")

ROI: front_side_view

OLD TOKENS:
{'labels': [], 'diameter': [], 'radius': [], 'angle': ['45°'], 'linear': ['26'], 'chamfer': ['6x45°']}

NEW TOKENS:
{'labels': [], 'diameter': [], 'radius': [], 'angle': ['45°'], 'linear': [], 'chamfer': ['6x45°']}

TOKEN CHANGE REPORT:

LINEAR:
  removed: ['26']

STEP 54 — Generate Comprehensive ROI Engineering Change Report¶

In [120]:
# Purpose:
#   Build a structured per-ROI report using OCR text, engineering tokens,
#   token comparison, suspicion flags, and confidence assessment.
# Inputs:
#   candidate_view_rois, view_roi_crops, roi_change_stats
# Outputs:
#   final_view_report dictionary

final_view_report = {}

for roi_name in candidate_view_rois:
    roi_data = view_roi_crops.get(roi_name, {})
    old_img = roi_data.get("old", None)
    new_img = roi_data.get("new", None)

    if old_img is None or new_img is None:
        final_view_report[roi_name] = {
            "error": "Missing old/new ROI image",
            "pixel_change_percent": roi_change_stats.get(roi_name, 0.0),
        }
        continue

    old_texts = ocr_view_tokens(old_img)
    new_texts = ocr_view_tokens(new_img)

    old_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(old_texts)
    new_tokens_raw = extract_engineering_tokens(new_texts)

    old_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(old_tokens_raw)
    new_tokens = filter_dimension_noise(new_tokens_raw)

    token_report = compare_token_sets(old_tokens, new_tokens)

    old_flags = symbol_suspicion_flags(old_texts)
    new_flags = symbol_suspicion_flags(new_texts)

    confidence_report = assess_token_change_confidence(token_report, old_texts, new_texts)

    final_view_report[roi_name] = {
        "pixel_change_percent": roi_change_stats.get(roi_name, 0.0),
        "old_texts_preview": old_texts[:5] if old_texts else [],
        "new_texts_preview": new_texts[:5] if new_texts else [],
        "old_tokens": old_tokens,
        "new_tokens": new_tokens,
        "token_changes": token_report,
        "old_flags": old_flags,
        "new_flags": new_flags,
        "status": confidence_report["status"],
        "confidence": confidence_report["confidence"],
        "remark": confidence_report["remark"],
    }

print("STEP 54 complete.")
print(list(final_view_report.keys()))

STEP 54 complete.
['section_view', 'front_face_view', 'front_side_view', 'iso_view']

STEP 55 — Validate Final ROI Report Structure¶

In [71]:
# Purpose:
#   Validate report structure for all processed ROIs.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed validation summary

required_keys = {
    "pixel_change_percent",
    "old_tokens",
    "new_tokens",
    "token_changes",
    "old_flags",
    "new_flags",
    "status",
    "confidence",
    "remark",
}

print("\nVALIDATION SUMMARY")
print("=" * 60)

for roi_name, info in final_view_report.items():
    if "error" in info:
        print(f"{roi_name}: ERROR -> {info['error']}")
        continue

    is_valid = required_keys.issubset(info.keys())
    print(f"{roi_name}: VALID = {is_valid}")

VALIDATION SUMMARY

front_face_view: VALID = True
front_side_view: VALID = True
iso_view: VALID = True

STEP 56 — Generate Human-Readable Engineering Change Summary¶

In [72]:
# Purpose:
#   Produce a readable summary of engineering token differences
#   detected across candidate view ROIs.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed engineering change summary

print("\nENGINEERING CHANGE SUMMARY")
print("=" * 70)

for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():

    if "error" in data:
        print(f"\nROI: {roi_name}")
        print("  ERROR:", data["error"])
        continue

    pixel_pct = data.get("pixel_change_percent", 0.0)
    token_changes = data.get("token_changes", {})
    old_flags = data.get("old_flags", {})
    new_flags = data.get("new_flags", {})

    status = data.get("status", "UNKNOWN")
    confidence = data.get("confidence", "UNKNOWN")

    print(f"\nROI: {roi_name}")
    print(f"Pixel Change % : {pixel_pct:.2f}%")
    print(f"Status         : {status}")
    print(f"Confidence     : {confidence}")

    found_any = False

    for token_type, change in token_changes.items():

        added = change.get("added", [])
        removed = change.get("removed", [])

        if added or removed:
            found_any = True
            print(f"  {token_type.upper()}:")

            if added:
                print(f"    added   : {added}")

            if removed:
                print(f"    removed : {removed}")

    if old_flags.get("section_label_suspected") and not new_flags.get("section_label_suspected"):
        found_any = True
        print("  SECTION LABEL CHANGE:")
        print("    removed : section label suspected")

    if (not old_flags.get("view_label_suspected")) and new_flags.get("view_label_suspected"):
        found_any = True
        print("  VIEW LABEL CHANGE:")
        print("    added   : view label suspected")

    if not found_any:
        print("  No clear engineering token change detected")

ENGINEERING CHANGE SUMMARY
======================================================================

Pixel Change % : 0.06%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
  LABELS:
    removed : ['A-A']
  LINEAR:
    added   : ['979']
  SECTION LABEL CHANGE:
    removed : section label suspected

ROI: front_face_view
Pixel Change % : 0.21%
Status         : LIKELY TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : MEDIUM
  LABELS:
    added   : ['B(1:5)']
  VIEW LABEL CHANGE:
    added   : view label suspected

ROI: front_side_view
Pixel Change % : 0.17%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
  LINEAR:
    removed : ['26']

ROI: iso_view
Pixel Change % : 0.11%
Status         : NO TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : HIGH
  No clear engineering token change detected

STEP 57 — Clean OCR Linear Dimension Tokens (Noise Filtering)¶

In [73]:
# Purpose:
#   Remove unrealistic or noisy numeric tokens detected by OCR.
# Inputs:
#   tokens -> list of numeric dimension strings
# Outputs:
#   cleaned list of linear tokens

def clean_linear_tokens(tokens):

    if tokens is None:
        return []

    clean = []

    for t in tokens:
        try:
            v = float(t)

            # ignore very small OCR noise
            if v < 5:
                continue

            # ignore unrealistic large dimensions
            if v > 2000:
                continue

            clean.append(t)

        except:
            # ignore values that cannot be parsed
            continue

    return clean

STEP 58 — Test Linear Token Cleaning Function¶

In [74]:
test_tokens = ["1", "3", "5", "26", "2500", "12.5", "abc"]

print("INPUT :", test_tokens)
print("OUTPUT:", clean_linear_tokens(test_tokens))

INPUT : ['1', '3', '5', '26', '2500', '12.5', 'abc']
OUTPUT: ['5', '26', '12.5']

STEP 59 — Detect Likely Dimension Modifications from Token Changes¶

In [75]:
# Purpose:
#   Detect likely dimension modifications by pairing removed and added
#   linear tokens that are numerically close.
# Inputs:
#   token_changes -> token comparison report
# Outputs:
#   list of likely dimension modification strings

def detect_dimension_modifications(token_changes, tolerance=10.0):

    modifications = []

    if token_changes is None:
        return modifications

    added = token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("added", [])
    removed = token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("removed", [])

    added = clean_linear_tokens(added)
    removed = clean_linear_tokens(removed)

    used_added = set()

    for r in removed:
        try:
            r_val = float(r)
        except:
            continue

        best_match = None
        best_diff = None

        for a in added:
            if a in used_added:
                continue

            try:
                a_val = float(a)
            except:
                continue

            diff = abs(r_val - a_val)

            if diff <= tolerance:
                if best_diff is None or diff < best_diff:
                    best_diff = diff
                    best_match = a

        if best_match is not None:
            modifications.append(f"{r} → {best_match}")
            used_added.add(best_match)

    return modifications

STEP 60 — Test Dimension Modification Detection¶

In [76]:
sample_token_changes = {
    "linear": {
        "removed": ["25", "100", "12.5"],
        "added": ["26", "108", "13.0"],
        "common": []
    }
}

mods = detect_dimension_modifications(sample_token_changes, tolerance=10.0)

print("Detected modifications:")
print(mods)

Detected modifications:
['25 → 26', '100 → 108', '12.5 → 13.0']

STEP 61 — Generate Engineering-Friendly Change Summary with Dimension Modification Detection¶

In [77]:
# Purpose:
#   Print a more engineering-friendly summary including likely dimension
#   modifications, token additions/removals, and suspicion flag changes.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed engineering change summary

print("\nENGINEERING CHANGE SUMMARY")
print("=" * 70)

for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():

    if "error" in data:
        print(f"\nROI: {roi_name}")
        print("  ERROR:", data["error"])
        continue

    pixel_pct = data.get("pixel_change_percent", 0.0)
    token_changes = data.get("token_changes", {})
    old_flags = data.get("old_flags", {})
    new_flags = data.get("new_flags", {})
    status = data.get("status", "UNKNOWN")
    confidence = data.get("confidence", "UNKNOWN")

    print(f"\nROI: {roi_name}")
    print(f"Pixel Change % : {pixel_pct:.2f}%")
    print(f"Status         : {status}")
    print(f"Confidence     : {confidence}")

    found_any = False

    # detect likely dimension modifications
    mods = detect_dimension_modifications(token_changes)

    mod_removed = set()
    mod_added = set()

    if mods:
        found_any = True
        print("  DIMENSION MODIFIED:")
        for m in mods:
            print(f"    {m}")
            if "→" in m:
                left, right = m.split("→")
                mod_removed.add(left.strip())
                mod_added.add(right.strip())

    for token_type, change in token_changes.items():

        added = change.get("added", [])
        removed = change.get("removed", [])

        if token_type == "linear":
            added = clean_linear_tokens(added)
            removed = clean_linear_tokens(removed)

            # suppress values already reported as modifications
            added = [x for x in added if x not in mod_added]
            removed = [x for x in removed if x not in mod_removed]

        if added or removed:
            found_any = True
            print(f"  {token_type.upper()}:")

            if added:
                print(f"    added   : {added}")

            if removed:
                print(f"    removed : {removed}")

    if old_flags.get("section_label_suspected") and not new_flags.get("section_label_suspected"):
        found_any = True
        print("  SECTION LABEL CHANGE:")
        print("    removed : section label suspected")

    if (not old_flags.get("view_label_suspected")) and new_flags.get("view_label_suspected"):
        found_any = True
        print("  VIEW LABEL CHANGE:")
        print("    added   : view label suspected")

    if not found_any:
        print("  No clear engineering token change detected")

ENGINEERING CHANGE SUMMARY
======================================================================

ROI: section_view
Pixel Change % : 0.06%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
  LABELS:
    removed : ['A-A']
  LINEAR:
    added   : ['979']
  SECTION LABEL CHANGE:
    removed : section label suspected

ROI: front_face_view
Pixel Change % : 0.21%
Status         : LIKELY TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : MEDIUM
  LABELS:
    added   : ['B(1:5)']
  VIEW LABEL CHANGE:
    added   : view label suspected

ROI: front_side_view
Pixel Change % : 0.17%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
  LINEAR:
    removed : ['26']

ROI: iso_view
Pixel Change % : 0.11%
Status         : NO TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : HIGH
  No clear engineering token change detected

STEP 62 — Compute ROI Change Detection Statistics¶

In [78]:
# Purpose:
#   Count how many ROIs contain detected change content.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed summary counts

roi_with_changes = 0
roi_without_changes = 0
roi_with_errors = 0

for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():
    if "error" in data:
        roi_with_errors += 1
        continue

    token_changes = data.get("token_changes", {})
    mods = detect_dimension_modifications(token_changes)

    found_any = False

    if mods:
        found_any = True

    for token_type, change in token_changes.items():
        added = change.get("added", [])
        removed = change.get("removed", [])
        if token_type == "linear":
            added = clean_linear_tokens(added)
            removed = clean_linear_tokens(removed)
        if added or removed:
            found_any = True
            break

    if found_any:
        roi_with_changes += 1
    else:
        roi_without_changes += 1

print("ROIs with detected changes :", roi_with_changes)
print("ROIs without changes       :", roi_without_changes)
print("ROIs with errors           :", roi_with_errors)

ROIs with detected changes : 3
ROIs without changes       : 1
ROIs with errors           : 0

STEP 63 — Suppress Linear Dimension Noise During Label/View Changes¶

In [79]:
# Purpose:
#   Reduce false linear-token alarms when the main detected event
#   is likely a section/view label change.
# Inputs:
#   roi_name, token_changes, old_flags, new_flags
# Outputs:
#   cleaned token_changes dictionary

def suppress_linear_noise_for_roi(roi_name, token_changes, old_flags, new_flags):
    cleaned = {}

    if token_changes is None:
        return {}

    # preserve full structure safely
    for token_type, change in token_changes.items():
        cleaned[token_type] = {
            "added": list(change.get("added", [])),
            "removed": list(change.get("removed", [])),
            "common": list(change.get("common", []))
        }

    strong_label_change = (
        (old_flags.get("section_label_suspected", False) and not new_flags.get("section_label_suspected", False)) or
        ((not old_flags.get("view_label_suspected", False)) and new_flags.get("view_label_suspected", False))
    )

    if strong_label_change and "linear" in cleaned:
        cleaned["linear"]["added"] = clean_linear_tokens(cleaned["linear"]["added"])
        cleaned["linear"]["removed"] = clean_linear_tokens(cleaned["linear"]["removed"])

        # suppress isolated weak linear noise when label change is already the stronger event
        if len(cleaned["linear"]["added"]) <= 1 and len(cleaned["linear"]["removed"]) == 0:
            cleaned["linear"]["added"] = []

        if len(cleaned["linear"]["removed"]) <= 1 and len(cleaned["linear"]["added"]) == 0:
            cleaned["linear"]["removed"] = []

    return cleaned

STEP 64 — Test Linear Noise Suppression for Label/View Change Scenarios¶

In [80]:
sample_token_changes = {
    "labels": {"added": [], "removed": ["A-A"], "common": []},
    "linear": {"added": ["979"], "removed": [], "common": []},
    "angle": {"added": [], "removed": [], "common": ["45°"]},
    "chamfer": {"added": [], "removed": [], "common": ["6x45°"]}
}

sample_old_flags = {
    "section_label_suspected": True,
    "view_label_suspected": False
}

sample_new_flags = {
    "section_label_suspected": False,
    "view_label_suspected": False
}

cleaned_changes = suppress_linear_noise_for_roi(
    roi_name="section_view",
    token_changes=sample_token_changes,
    old_flags=sample_old_flags,
    new_flags=sample_new_flags
)

print("BEFORE:")
print(sample_token_changes)

print("\nAFTER:")
print(cleaned_changes)

BEFORE:
{'labels': {'added': [], 'removed': ['A-A'], 'common': []}, 'linear': {'added': ['979'], 'removed': [], 'common': []}, 'angle': {'added': [], 'removed': [], 'common': ['45°']}, 'chamfer': {'added': [], 'removed': [], 'common': ['6x45°']}}

AFTER:
{'labels': {'added': [], 'removed': ['A-A'], 'common': []}, 'linear': {'added': [], 'removed': [], 'common': []}, 'angle': {'added': [], 'removed': [], 'common': ['45°']}, 'chamfer': {'added': [], 'removed': [], 'common': ['6x45°']}}

STEP 65 — Generate Final Compact Engineering Change Report¶

In [81]:
# Purpose:
#   Print a compact final-style engineering report using cleaned token changes,
#   likely dimension modifications, and confidence-aware wording.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed final engineering change report

print("\nFINAL ENGINEERING CHANGE REPORT")
print("=" * 70)

for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():

    print(f"\nROI: {roi_name}")

    if "error" in data:
        print(" - ERROR:", data["error"])
        continue

    pixel_pct = data.get("pixel_change_percent", 0.0)
    status = data.get("status", "UNKNOWN")
    confidence = data.get("confidence", "UNKNOWN")

    token_changes = suppress_linear_noise_for_roi(
        roi_name,
        data.get("token_changes", {}),
        data.get("old_flags", {}),
        data.get("new_flags", {})
    )

    change_lines = []

    # detect likely linear modifications first
    mods = detect_dimension_modifications(token_changes)
    mod_removed = set()
    mod_added = set()

    for m in mods:
        change_lines.append(f"Likely dimension modified: {m}")
        if "→" in m:
            left, right = m.split("→")
            mod_removed.add(left.strip())
            mod_added.add(right.strip())

    # label changes
    for v in token_changes.get("labels", {}).get("added", []):
        if confidence == "LOW":
            change_lines.append(f"Possible view label added: {v}")
        else:
            change_lines.append(f"View label added: {v}")

    for v in token_changes.get("labels", {}).get("removed", []):
        if confidence == "LOW":
            change_lines.append(f"Possible label removed: {v}")
        else:
            change_lines.append(f"Label removed: {v}")

    # dimension changes (exclude ones already reported as modifications)
    linear_removed = [
        v for v in token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("removed", [])
        if v not in mod_removed
    ]
    linear_added = [
        v for v in token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("added", [])
        if v not in mod_added
    ]

    for v in linear_removed:
        if confidence == "LOW":
            change_lines.append(f"Possible dimension removed: {v}")
        else:
            change_lines.append(f"Dimension removed: {v}")

    for v in linear_added:
        if confidence == "LOW":
            change_lines.append(f"Possible dimension added: {v}")
        else:
            change_lines.append(f"Dimension added: {v}")

    print(f"Pixel Change % : {pixel_pct:.2f}%")
    print(f"Status         : {status}")
    print(f"Confidence     : {confidence}")

    if change_lines:
        for c in change_lines:
            print(" -", c)
    else:
        print(" - No engineering change detected")

FINAL ENGINEERING CHANGE REPORT
======================================================================

ROI: section_view
Pixel Change % : 0.06%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
 - Possible label removed: A-A

ROI: front_face_view
Pixel Change % : 0.21%
Status         : LIKELY TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : MEDIUM
 - View label added: B(1:5)

ROI: front_side_view
Pixel Change % : 0.17%
Status         : POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : LOW
 - Possible dimension removed: 26

ROI: iso_view
Pixel Change % : 0.11%
Status         : NO TOKEN CHANGE
Confidence     : HIGH
 - No engineering change detected

STEP 66 — Compute Final ROI Change Reporting Coverage¶

In [82]:
# Purpose:
#   Count ROIs with detected output lines after cleaning/suppression.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report
# Outputs:
#   printed coverage summary

roi_with_output = 0
roi_without_output = 0
roi_with_error = 0

for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():

    if "error" in data:
        roi_with_error += 1
        continue

    token_changes = suppress_linear_noise_for_roi(
        roi_name,
        data.get("token_changes", {}),
        data.get("old_flags", {}),
        data.get("new_flags", {})
    )

    mods = detect_dimension_modifications(token_changes)
    has_output = bool(mods)

    for token_type, change in token_changes.items():
        added = change.get("added", [])
        removed = change.get("removed", [])
        if added or removed:
            has_output = True
            break

    if has_output:
        roi_with_output += 1
    else:
        roi_without_output += 1

print("ROIs with reportable changes :", roi_with_output)
print("ROIs without changes         :", roi_without_output)
print("ROIs with errors             :", roi_with_error)

ROIs with reportable changes : 3
ROIs without changes         : 1
ROIs with errors             : 0

Validation¶

STEP 67 — Define Ground Truth Engineering Changes for Evaluation¶

In [119]:
# Purpose:
#   Define the expected engineering changes manually for evaluation.
# Inputs:
#   Known edits in this drawing pair
# Outputs:
#   ground_truth dictionary

ground_truth = {
    "section_view": {
        "changed": True,
        "change_type": "label_removed",
        "tokens_added": [],
        "tokens_removed": ["A-A"],
        "expected_pixel_change": True,
        "notes": "Section label A-A removed"
    },
    "front_face_view": {
        "changed": True,
        "change_type": "label_added",
        "tokens_added": ["B(1:5)"],
        "tokens_removed": [],
        "expected_pixel_change": True,
        "notes": "View label B(1:5) added"
    },
    "front_side_view": {
        "changed": True,
        "change_type": "dimension_removed",
        "tokens_added": [],
        "tokens_removed": ["26"],
        "expected_pixel_change": True,
        "notes": "Linear dimension 26 removed"
    },
    "iso_view": {
        "changed": False,
        "change_type": "no_change",
        "tokens_added": [],
        "tokens_removed": [],
        "expected_pixel_change": False,
        "notes": "No intended engineering change"
    },
    "notes_block": {
        "changed": True,
        "change_type": "notes_added",
        "tokens_added": [],
        "tokens_removed": [],
        "expected_pixel_change": True,
        "notes": "Notes text added/changed"
    }
}

print("STEP 67 complete.")
print(ground_truth)

STEP 67 complete.
{'section_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'label_removed', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': ['A-A'], 'expected_pixel_change': True, 'notes': 'Section label A-A removed'}, 'front_face_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'label_added', 'tokens_added': ['B(1:5)'], 'tokens_removed': [], 'expected_pixel_change': True, 'notes': 'View label B(1:5) added'}, 'front_side_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'dimension_removed', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': ['26'], 'expected_pixel_change': True, 'notes': 'Linear dimension 26 removed'}, 'iso_view': {'changed': False, 'change_type': 'no_change', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': [], 'expected_pixel_change': False, 'notes': 'No intended engineering change'}, 'notes_block': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'notes_added', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': [], 'expected_pixel_change': True, 'notes': 'Notes text added/changed'}}

STEP 68 — Validate Ground Truth Schema for All ROIs¶

In [93]:
# Purpose:
#   Check that ground_truth has the expected schema for all ROIs.
# Inputs:
#   ground_truth
# Outputs:
#   printed validation results

required_keys = {
    "changed",
    "change_type",
    "tokens_added",
    "tokens_removed",
    "expected_pixel_change",
    "notes"
}

print("GROUND TRUTH VALIDATION")
print("=" * 60)

for roi_name, info in ground_truth.items():
    missing = required_keys - set(info.keys())
    valid = len(missing) == 0

    print(f"{roi_name}: VALID = {valid}")
    if not valid:
        print("  Missing keys:", missing)

GROUND TRUTH VALIDATION
============================================================
section_view: VALID = True
front_face_view: VALID = True
front_side_view: VALID = True
iso_view: VALID = True
notes_block: VALID = True

STEP 69 — Build OCR Notes Evaluation Summary¶

In [117]:
# Purpose:
#   Build a stable evaluation summary for OCR-friendly notes ROIs.
# Inputs:
#   OCR_ROIS, ocr_output_old, ocr_output_new, similarity()
# Outputs:
#   notes_eval_rows list of dictionaries

NOTES_SAME_THRESHOLD = 95.0

notes_eval_rows = []

for k in OCR_ROIS:
    old_t = str(ocr_output_old.get(k, "") or "").strip()
    new_t = str(ocr_output_new.get(k, "") or "").strip()

    if not old_t and not new_t:
        status = "NO NOTES / NO CHANGE"
        sim_pct = 100.0
    elif not old_t and new_t:
        status = "NOTES ADDED"
        sim_pct = 0.0
    elif old_t and not new_t:
        status = "NOTES REMOVED"
        sim_pct = 0.0
    else:
        sim = similarity(old_t, new_t)
        sim_pct = round(sim * 100, 2)
        status = "NOTES SAME" if sim_pct >= NOTES_SAME_THRESHOLD else "NOTES MODIFIED"

    notes_eval_rows.append({
        "roi_name": k,
        "similarity_percent": sim_pct,
        "status": status,
        "old_text_preview": old_t[:120],
        "new_text_preview": new_t[:120]
    })

print("STEP 69 complete.")
print(notes_eval_rows)

STEP 69 complete.
[{'roi_name': 'notes_block', 'similarity_percent': 0.0, 'status': 'NOTES ADDED', 'old_text_preview': '', 'new_text_preview': '1. Read this drawing with previous number\n2. Do not weld should be done at shop'}]

STEP 70 — Validate Notes Evaluation Row Structure¶

In [95]:
# Purpose:
#   Check that each notes evaluation row has required fields.
# Inputs:
#   notes_eval_rows
# Outputs:
#   printed validation summary

required_keys = {
    "roi_name",
    "similarity_percent",
    "status",
    "old_text_preview",
    "new_text_preview"
}

print("NOTES EVALUATION VALIDATION")
print("=" * 60)

for row in notes_eval_rows:
    missing = required_keys - set(row.keys())
    valid = len(missing) == 0
    print(f"{row.get('roi_name', 'UNKNOWN')}: VALID = {valid}")
    if not valid:
        print("  Missing keys:", missing)

NOTES EVALUATION VALIDATION
============================================================
notes_block: VALID = True

STEP 71 — Convert Pipeline Outputs into Predicted Evaluation Results¶

In [116]:
# Purpose:
#   Convert current pipeline outputs into a standard evaluation dictionary.
# Inputs:
#   final_view_report, notes_eval_rows
# Outputs:
#   predicted_results dictionary

predicted_results = {}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# PART A: View / annotation ROIs from final_view_report
# ---------------------------------------------------------
for roi_name, data in final_view_report.items():

    if "error" in data:
        predicted_results[roi_name] = {
            "changed": False,
            "change_type": "error",
            "tokens_added": [],
            "tokens_removed": [],
            "status": "ERROR"
        }
        continue

    token_changes = suppress_linear_noise_for_roi(
        roi_name,
        data.get("token_changes", {}),
        data.get("old_flags", {}),
        data.get("new_flags", {})
    )

    labels_added = token_changes.get("labels", {}).get("added", [])
    labels_removed = token_changes.get("labels", {}).get("removed", [])
    linear_added = clean_linear_tokens(token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("added", []))
    linear_removed = clean_linear_tokens(token_changes.get("linear", {}).get("removed", []))

    changed = False
    change_type = "no_change"
    tokens_added = []
    tokens_removed = []

    # priority-based prediction
    if labels_removed:
        changed = True
        change_type = "label_removed"
        tokens_removed.extend(labels_removed)

    elif labels_added:
        changed = True
        change_type = "label_added"
        tokens_added.extend(labels_added)

    elif linear_removed:
        changed = True
        change_type = "dimension_removed"
        tokens_removed.extend(linear_removed)

    elif linear_added:
        changed = True
        change_type = "dimension_added"
        tokens_added.extend(linear_added)

    predicted_results[roi_name] = {
        "changed": changed,
        "change_type": change_type,
        "tokens_added": tokens_added,
        "tokens_removed": tokens_removed,
        "status": data.get("status", "UNKNOWN"),
        "confidence": data.get("confidence", "UNKNOWN")
    }

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# PART B: Notes ROI results from notes_eval_rows
# ---------------------------------------------------------
for row in notes_eval_rows:
    roi_name = row.get("roi_name", "UNKNOWN")
    status = row.get("status", "UNKNOWN")

    if status == "NOTES ADDED":
        predicted_results[roi_name] = {
            "changed": True,
            "change_type": "notes_added",
            "tokens_added": [],
            "tokens_removed": [],
            "status": status,
            "confidence": "HIGH"
        }

    elif status == "NOTES REMOVED":
        predicted_results[roi_name] = {
            "changed": True,
            "change_type": "notes_removed",
            "tokens_added": [],
            "tokens_removed": [],
            "status": status,
            "confidence": "HIGH"
        }

    elif status == "NOTES MODIFIED":
        predicted_results[roi_name] = {
            "changed": True,
            "change_type": "notes_modified",
            "tokens_added": [],
            "tokens_removed": [],
            "status": status,
            "confidence": "MEDIUM"
        }

    else:
        predicted_results[roi_name] = {
            "changed": False,
            "change_type": "no_change",
            "tokens_added": [],
            "tokens_removed": [],
            "status": status,
            "confidence": "HIGH"
        }

print("STEP 71 complete.")
print(predicted_results)

STEP 71 complete.
{'section_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'label_removed', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': ['A-A'], 'status': 'POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE', 'confidence': 'LOW'}, 'front_face_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'label_added', 'tokens_added': ['B(1:5)'], 'tokens_removed': [], 'status': 'LIKELY TOKEN CHANGE', 'confidence': 'MEDIUM'}, 'front_side_view': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'dimension_removed', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': ['26'], 'status': 'POSSIBLE TOKEN CHANGE', 'confidence': 'LOW'}, 'iso_view': {'changed': False, 'change_type': 'no_change', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': [], 'status': 'NO TOKEN CHANGE', 'confidence': 'HIGH'}, 'notes_block': {'changed': True, 'change_type': 'notes_added', 'tokens_added': [], 'tokens_removed': [], 'status': 'NOTES ADDED', 'confidence': 'HIGH'}}
